name: OmopPatientPerson
title: OMOP-Convertible Patient → person
description: FHIR Patient that can be converted to OMOP `person`. Requires birthDate (used for year/month/day_of_birth), gender (mapped to gender_concept_id), and at least one identifier (used as person_source_value). The discriminator is birthDate presence — Patient has no `code` element, so no domain ValueSet binding applies.

element.comment: OMOP-native race (preferred when source already speaks OMOP). valueCoding.code = Athena Race concept_id; copied verbatim into person.race_concept_id. If both omopRace and us-core-race are present, omopRace wins in the ETL.

OMOP-native ethnicity (preferred when source already speaks OMOP). valueCoding.code = Athena Ethnicity concept_id (38003563 / 38003564); copied verbatim into person.ethnicity_concept_id. If both omopEthnicity and us-core-ethnicity are present, omopEthnicity wins.

US Core Race (fallback when omopRace is absent). ombCategory.code → race_source_value; text → race_source_value fallback. Mapped to OMOP race_concept_id via cm.race_omb_to_omop (+ cm.race_text_synthea_to_omop for Synthea's UNK+text='Other' quirk).
US Core Ethnicity (fallback when omopEthnicity is absent). ombCategory.code → ethnicity_source_value. Mapped to OMOP ethnicity_concept_id via cm.ethnicity_omb_to_omop.
US Core Birth Sex (M | F | OTH | UNK). Used as gender_source_value when present; otherwise Patient.gender is used.

binding.description: male | female | other | unknown. Mapped to gender_concept_id (8507/8532/8521/8551).

element.comment: Required — drives person.year_of_birth (and month/day/birth_datetime when components are present). This is the routing discriminator for Patient → person.
Optional. When present, drives the Patient → location edge and person.location_id.
Optional. First Practitioner reference becomes person.provider_id.
